info.title: GraphClaw Gateway
info.description: Channel gateway for the GraphClaw task graph orchestration system. Accepts inbound messages (email, API, CLI), queues outbound notifications, and exposes health/readiness probes.

post.description:
Accept an inbound message and publish it to the broker queue.

Parameters
----------
message:
    Normalised ``InboundMessage`` payload submitted by the caller.
broker:
    ``MessageBroker`` injected via ``Depends``.

Returns
-------
dict[str, str]:
    ``{"status": "accepted", "message_id": <id>}`` on success.

Raises
------
HTTPException (500):
    If broker publishing fails due to an unexpected error.

schema.title: Response Receive Message Api V1 Inbound Messages Post

post.description:
Queue an outbound message for delivery.

Parameters
----------
message:
    ``OutboundMessage`` payload to enqueue.
broker:
    ``MessageBroker`` injected via ``Depends``.

Returns
-------
dict[str, str]:
    ``{"status": "queued", "message_id": <id>}`` on success.

Raises
------
HTTPException (500):
    If broker publishing fails due to an unexpected error.

schema.title: Response Send Message Api V1 Outbound Messages Post

get.description: Redirects the browser to the chosen identity provider's authorization page. The ``provider`` query parameter must be one of ``google``, ``github``, or ``microsoft``.  The IdP must be configured via the corresponding ``OAUTH_<PROVIDER>_CLIENT_ID`` and ``OAUTH_<PROVIDER>_CLIENT_SECRET`` environment variables.

get.summary: OAuth 2.0 callback — exchange code for tokens
get.description: Receives the IdP authorization callback, validates the CSRF state, exchanges the authorization code for an access token, fetches the user profile, provisions a UserNode if needed, and returns GraphClaw access + refresh tokens.

post.description: Exchanges a short-lived one-time code (issued by the OAuth callback redirect) for a real access + refresh token pair. The code is deleted after first use and expires in 30 seconds.
Validates the supplied refresh token, revokes it, and issues a fresh access + refresh token pair.  The old refresh token is immediately invalidated to prevent reuse (refresh token rotation).

Revokes the supplied refresh token, preventing it from being used to obtain new access tokens.  Access tokens are short-lived (15 min) and expire naturally; they cannot be revoked individually via this endpoint.

post.summary: Issue a dev JWT (development mode only)
post.description: Issues a real RS256 JWT for the given ``user_id`` without OAuth. **Only available when ``ENVIRONMENT=development``.**  Returns HTTP 403 in production.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's platform ID and profile. Requires a valid Bearer access token in the ``Authorization`` header.
Returns all active agent registrations for the authenticated user.  Only agents with an active (non-revoked) key are included.  Plaintext keys are never returned.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 A2A Agents Get

post.description: Creates a new agent registration and issues an API key in ``wg_agent_*`` format.  The plaintext key is returned **once** in this response — it is never stored or re-transmitted.  Store it securely immediately.

schema.title: Response Register Agent Api V1 A2A Agents Post

post.description: Generates a new API key for the specified agent registration and immediately invalidates the previous key.  The new plaintext key is returned **once** — store it securely.

delete.description: Clears the API key hash from the agent registration, immediately preventing any further authentication with that key.  The agent registration node is retained but can no longer authenticate requests.

post.description: Main inbound endpoint for Agent-to-Agent task updates.  Authenticated via ``X-Agent-Api-Key`` header.  Body must be a JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope (``A2AUpdatePayload``).  The ``params`` dict is forwarded to the agent loop via the broker queue.  Returns HTTP 202 immediately; processing is asynchronous.  Requests exceeding ``A2A_MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_KB`` (default 512 KB) are rejected with HTTP 413.

schema.title: Response Task Update Api V1 Task Update Post

get.description: Return the authenticated user's application settings including LLM provider, timezone, and channel configuration.

patch.description: Partially update the authenticated user's settings.  Only supplied fields are changed; omitted fields retain their current values.

get.description: Return all channels configured for the authenticated user with their enabled/disabled status.

schema.title: Response List Channels App V1 Settings Channels Get

get.description: Return the authenticated user's profile from the graph store.

patch.description: Partially update the authenticated user's profile in the graph store.

post.description: Add or re-enable a channel for the authenticated user.  If the channel already exists it is updated to enabled=True.

delete.description: Disable a channel for the authenticated user.  The channel entry is retained with enabled=False so its configuration is preserved.

get.description: Return the user's current 7-factor scoring weights (W1–W7).

patch.description: Partially update the user's scoring weights.  Only supplied factors are changed; omitted factors retain their current values.

get.description: Return all organizations the authenticated user is a member of.

schema.title: Response List Organizations App V1 Settings Organizations Get

post.description: Create a new organization owned by the authenticated user.

patch.description: Update name or domain of an organization owned by the authenticated user.

post.description: Store an LLM provider API key in the secrets backend.  The key value is never returned; only the provider name is echoed back.

delete.description: Remove a stored LLM provider API key for the authenticated user.

get.description: Return all tasks in APPROVAL state (PENDING or IN_PROGRESS) for the authenticated user.  These are gated MCP tool calls awaiting human confirmation before execution.

schema.title: Response List Approvals App V1 Approvals Get

post.description: Approve an APPROVAL task, driving it to COMPLETE status via the state machine.  This permits the gated MCP tool call to proceed.
Deny an APPROVAL task, transitioning it to CANCELLED status.  The gated MCP tool call will not proceed.

get.description: Return all skills installed for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Skills App V1 Skills Get

get.description: Search for skills available across all registered sources (plus built-in LOCAL skills) by name, description, or tags.

schema.title: Response Search Skills App V1 Skills Search Get

post.description: Install a skill from a registered source URI.

get.description: Return all skill sources registered for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Sources App V1 Skills Sources Get

post.description: Register a new skill source.  Attempts to fetch the source index to populate last_fetched_at; saves the source regardless.

delete.description: Deregister a skill source by its URI.  Also uninstalls all skills from that source.

get.description: Return the status of all skill worker threads in the pool.  Returns an empty list when no worker pool is initialised.

schema.title: Response List Workers App V1 Skills Workers Get

get.description: Return detail for a single installed skill including per-skill config.

patch.description: Enable or disable an installed skill.

delete.description: Remove an installed skill by its skill_id.

post.description: Record a quality rating (0.0–1.0) for an installed skill.  Updates the EMA quality score and usage count via ``SkillRegistryService``.

get.description: Return the execution history for an installed skill.  History is stored by the skill worker at ``skills/executions/{user}/{skill}.json``.

schema.title: Response List Skill Executions App V1 Skills  Skill Id  Executions Get

post.description: Submit a test job for an installed skill.  Delegates to the worker pool when available; returns a mock submitted response when absent.

get.description: Return all MCP servers registered for the authenticated user (enabled and disabled).

schema.title: Response List Mcp Servers App V1 Mcp Servers Get

post.description: Register an MCP server in the authenticated user's personal registry.

get.description: Search for available MCP servers in the official registry at registry.modelcontextprotocol.io.  Returns an empty list if the upstream registry is unreachable.

schema.title: Response Search Mcp Registry App V1 Mcp Servers Search Get

get.description: Return the details of a registered MCP server by its ID.

patch.summary: Update MCP server trust tier or enabled status
patch.description: Partially update a registered MCP server's trust tier or enabled flag.  Note: BLOCKED servers cannot be promoted directly to AUTO — set to GATED first.

delete.description: Remove a registered MCP server from the user's personal registry.  If the server has a secret_ref, the associated credential is also deleted.

get.description: Return the tools advertised by the registered MCP server.  Attempts a live connection and degrades gracefully to an empty list if the server is unreachable.

schema.title: Response List Mcp Server Tools App V1 Mcp Servers  Server Id  Tools Get

get.description: Return all A2A agents registered by the authenticated user.  Plaintext keys are never returned after initial registration.

schema.title: Response List Agents App V1 A2A Agents Get

post.description: Register a new agent and return its API key in plaintext.  The key is disclosed only once — store it securely.
Issue a new API key for the agent, invalidating the previous one.  The new key is returned in plaintext once only.

delete.description: Permanently revoke an A2A agent key, preventing further use.

get.description: Trigger a full export of the authenticated user's data. Returns the export ID, S3 storage key, and expiry timestamp. Use the storage key to generate a presigned download URL.

post.description: Submit a GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure request for the authenticated user. The request is queued for processing and a request_id is returned for status polling.

get.description: Poll the status of a previously submitted erasure request.
Return all GoalNodes visible to the authenticated user.  Optionally filter by organisation or goal state.
Return all nodes reachable from this goal (tasks, constraints, resources) and the edges that connect them, up to ``depth`` hops.
Return TaskNodes matching the given filters.  Supports filtering by state, assignee, organisation, goal, and task type.

post.description: Create a new TaskNode in the graph.

schema.title: Response Create Task App V1 Graph Tasks Post

get.description: Return a single TaskNode with its latest score block and all incident edges.

patch.description: Partial-update a TaskNode.  Only provided fields are changed.

schema.title: Response Update Task App V1 Graph Tasks  Task Id  Patch

delete.description: Delete a TaskNode and all its incident edges (DETACH DELETE).

get.description: Return ResourceNodes (people and AI agents) visible to the authenticated user.
Return graph edges, optionally filtered by type, source node, or target node.

post.description: Create a directed edge between two nodes.

schema.title: Response Create Edge App V1 Graph Edges Post

delete.description: Delete an edge by its ID.

get.description: Return the current 7-factor ScoreExplanation for the given task. Data is read from the scoring block stored on the TaskNode during the last scoring pass.
Return paginated historical ScoreExplanations for a task. Currently returns the single latest scoring block; future passes will be appended as the scoring history log grows.

post.description: Re-compute the priority score for a task using hypothetical weights or factor overrides.  Useful for the cockpit's 'what-if' slider UI.

get.description: Return the full audit trail of state transitions for the given task, ordered oldest-first. Each entry records from/to states, timestamp, who changed it, and an optional reason.
Return the list of target states the task can transition to from its current state, according to the transition table and domain guards.

post.description: Apply a human-initiated state transition to the task. The transition is validated against the state machine rules; invalid transitions return HTTP 422.

schema.title: Response Transition Task App V1 Tasks  Task Id  Transition Post

get.description: Return paginated inbound communication records for a user.
Return paginated outbound communication records for a user.
Open a Server-Sent Events connection to receive real-time graph change notifications.  Events are scoped to the authenticated user. If Redis is unavailable the stream emits keepalive pings only.
Return recent chat messages for the authenticated user, ordered oldest-first.  Supports cursor-based pagination.

post.description: Send a natural-language message to the GraphClaw agent and receive a response.  The message and response are appended to the user's chat history.

delete.description: Delete all chat messages for the authenticated user.

post.summary: Send a chat message and stream transparency events
post.description: Send a natural-language message and receive a ``text/event-stream`` response.  Each SSE frame carries one ``AgentRunEvent`` (delta text, tool calls, plan steps, etc.).  The run always ends with a ``run.completed`` or ``run.failed`` terminal event.  Chat history is persisted after the terminal event.

get.description: Return the full agent configuration for the authenticated user.  Returns an empty document for users who have not yet configured their agent.

put.description: Replace the entire agent configuration with the supplied document.  All existing settings are overwritten.

patch.description: Deep-merge the supplied partial config into the current config.  Nested dicts are recursively merged; supplied keys overwrite existing ones.

get.description: Return the reachability status of the configured secrets backend.
Return the names of all secrets known for the authenticated user.  Values are never returned — only key names.

schema.title: Response List Secrets App V1 Secrets Get

get.description: Return whether a secret with the given key exists for the authenticated user.

put.description: Create or overwrite a secret for the authenticated user.  The key is added to the user's secret index.

delete.description: Remove a secret by key for the authenticated user.

post.description: Verify that a secret is present and non-empty.  Does not make live API calls with the secret value.

get.description: Return the current running state, last scoring cycle timestamp, and approximate action queue depth.  Returns ``running: false`` when the agent loop is not initialised rather than raising 503.
Execute one scoring cycle and return the ranked task action queue. Delegates to ``AgentLoop.run_cycle()`` when available; returns an empty list when the agent loop is not initialised.

schema.title: Response Get Action Queue App V1 Agent Action Queue Get

get.description: Generate and return the 5-section daily briefing (critical, inferences, completed, ahead-of-curve, deferred) from the current graph state.
Return all triggers registered with the trigger engine, including their cron expressions and next scheduled fire times.  Returns an empty list when the trigger engine is not initialised.

schema.title: Response List Trigger Schedule App V1 Agent Triggers Schedule Get

get.description: Return the configuration and schedule for a specific trigger by ID.

post.description: Immediately dispatch an ON_DEMAND trigger event for the authenticated user.  The trigger_id is echoed in the payload so downstream consumers can correlate the event.
Disable an existing trigger until it is resumed.
Re-enable a snoozed trigger and recompute its next fire time.

get.description: Return chronological activity records from MinIO NDJSON logs for the authenticated user. Supports time-range filters, event-type filters, and cursor pagination.
Return session summaries aggregated from MinIO NDJSON logs for the authenticated user with offset pagination.
Return per-day inbound/outbound communication counters for the authenticated user by scanning historical NDJSON logs in object storage.
Return all agent canvas definitions owned by the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Agents App V1 Agents Get

post.description: Create a new agent canvas definition.

get.description: Return currently running sub-agent delegations from the in-memory SubAgentPool runner snapshots. Returns [] when the pool is not initialised.

schema.title: Response List Agent Delegations App V1 Agents Delegations Get

get.summary: Get dispatch plan tiers for a session
get.description: Return dispatch tier swim-lanes for a delegation session from the in-memory SubAgentPool planner snapshot. Returns an empty tier list when the pool is not initialised or no plan is registered.
Return a specific agent canvas definition by ID.

patch.description: Partially update an agent definition.  The previous version is automatically snapshotted before the update is written.

delete.description: Permanently delete an agent canvas definition and its version history. Pass ``?cleanup_runtime=true`` to also delete the runtime agent files (profile, config, manifest, memory).

get.description: Return the version history for an agent definition.

schema.title: Response List Agent Versions App V1 Agents  Agent Id  Versions Get

get.description: Return the runtime config.json for an agent (LLM model, tool wiring).

put.description: Write the runtime config.json for an agent (LLM model, tool wiring).

get.description: Return a resolved wiring summary for an agent: the skills, MCP servers, tool sets, and sub-agents currently configured in the runtime config.json. Skill and MCP server entries are enriched with metadata from the registries.

post.description: Run a quick test of the agent definition.  Delegates to ``AgentLoop.run_cycle()`` when available; returns a summary response with the current queue depth.

get.description: Return all pending MCP tool-call approval tasks for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Mcp Approvals App V1 Mcp Approvals Get

get.description: Return all members of the admin user's organization.

schema.title: Response List Members App V1 Admin Members Get

post.description: Send an invitation and add the member record to the organization.

patch.description: Update a member's role or status.

delete.description: Remove a member from the organization.

get.description: Return recent LLM evaluation results (most recent first).

schema.title: Response List Judge Results App V1 Admin Llm Judge Results Get

get.description: Return aggregated evaluation statistics.

post.description: Validate a rule set without persisting it.
Check whether a message would be blocked by the current rules.
Attempt to reach the SSO provider's discovery endpoint.

patch.description: Enable or disable mandatory SSO login for all org members.

get.description: Return audit log entries with optional filters for actor, action, resource type, and time window.  Results are ordered most-recent first.

schema.title: Response Query Audit Log App V1 Admin Audit Log Get

get.description: Return a stub deployment status (live data requires cloud probe).
Return a stub cluster health (live data requires infra probes).
Return backup inventory (stub — real data requires AWS/GCP API).

schema.title: Response List Backups App V1 Admin Backups Get
Response List Alarms App V1 Admin Alarms Get
Response List Migrations App V1 Admin Migrations Get

post.description: Apply pending schema migrations.  Set dry_run=true to preview without executing.

schema.title: Response List Connectors App V1 Admin Connectors Get

post.description:
Cancel a pending purge by writing purge_cancelled_at = now.

Sets purge_cancelled_at, clears archived_at and purge_after on the
subject node via admin_principal.  Writes an immutable audit entry.

Force a pending purge to execute immediately.

Moves purge_after to now - 1s so the purge worker picks it up on
the next tick.  Writes an immutable audit entry.

post.summary: GDPR Art.17 — immediate right to erasure (FR-DEL-006)
post.description:
Synchronous GDPR erasure — runs purge before response returns.

Validates re-auth recency, writes audit entry, marks node for
immediate purge, then runs a synchronous hard-archive of the
subject's graph substrate.

post.summary: Set legal hold on a node (FR-DEL-007)
post.description:
Place a legal hold on *node_id* to block purge worker.

Only admin_principal can set the hold.  Writes an immutable audit entry.

delete.summary: Release legal hold on a node (FR-DEL-007)
delete.description:
Release a legal hold, allowing the purge worker to proceed.

Writes an immutable audit entry with who released and when.

post.description:
Archive an org and all its workspaces — member UserNodes remain intact.

Acceptance criteria (FR-DEL-009):
- AC1: Members [USER-1, USER-2] are NOT archived.
- AC2: Workspaces are archived (tasks remain readable by admin).
Cancel a pending org archive by clearing archive fields.

get.description: Return all registered channel identity entries.

schema.title: Response List Entries App V1 Admin Agent Channels Get

post.description: Create a new channel identity mapping and hot-reload the registry.

put.description: Replace an existing channel identity mapping.

delete.description:
Deactivate (disable) a channel identity mapping.

Follows the no-delete principle: entry is marked inactive, not removed.

get.description: List current trigger configuration summary (admin only).

schema.title: Response List Trigger Config App V1 Admin Triggers Get

post.description:
Update follow-up trigger configuration for a user (FR-SCHED-001).

Persists ``default_follow_up_days`` and ``interrupt_threshold_overrides``
onto the user's ``UserNode.preferences``.

get.description: List pending decisions in the escalation queue for *user_id* (FR-SCHED-002).

schema.title: Response List Escalation Queue App V1 Admin Triggers Escalation  User Id  Get

post.description: Resolve a pending decision in the escalation queue (FR-SCHED-002).

schema.title: Response Resolve Escalation App V1 Admin Triggers Escalation  Decision Id  Resolve Post

get.description: List all agent ResourceNodes owned by the authenticated admin user.

schema.title: Response List Agents App V1 Admin Agents Get

post.description: Create a new AI agent ResourceNode for the authenticated admin user.

patch.description: Update the display name of an agent ResourceNode.

delete.description: Soft-archive an agent ResourceNode (sets link_status=ARCHIVED, does not delete).

post.description: Trigger a full or org-scoped sync of org_task_index against the AGE graph. Returns a reconciliation summary. (FR-AE-001)

get.description: Scan MinIO for all user-created agents plus accessible system agents.  Returns the agent_id, name, source (user/system), and description.  Used by the Intelligence Hub agent selector — NOT the canvas API.

schema.title: Response List Intelligence Agents App V1 Intelligence Agents Get

get.description: Return the Markdown profile document for the specified agent.  Returns a default empty profile when none has been written yet.

put.description: Write or replace the Markdown profile document for the specified agent.  The profile defines the agent's persona, goals, and working style.

get.description: Return the current-session working context for the agent.  This is a scratchpad overwritten by the agent loop on each cycle.

put.description: Write or replace the working context document.  Use this to manually inject context for the agent or to correct erroneous content.

post.description: Archive the current working context to episodic memory and replace it with a compact summary supplied by the caller.  Use this when the working context has grown too large or stale.  The original is preserved in episodic memory before being discarded.

get.description: Return metadata for all compacted working-context snapshots.  These are read-only — they are never loaded into the agent's live context.

schema.title: Response List Working Archive App V1 Intelligence Agents  Agent Id  Memory Working Archive Get

get.description: Return the combined character count of all memory that would be loaded into the agent's context: working context + active episodic entries + all semantic files.  Useful for the orchestrator's compact-at-60% rule.
Return all episodic memory entries (active and archived) for the agent.  Active entries are loaded into the agent's context; archived entries are excluded.

schema.title: Response List Episodic Memory App V1 Intelligence Agents  Agent Id  Memory Episodic Get

get.description: Return the content of one episodic memory entry (active or archived).

post.description: Move an active episodic entry to the archive.  Archived entries are excluded from the agent's context permanently.  This operation is irreversible.

get.description: Return all semantic memory topic names for the agent.
Return the content of one semantic memory topic.

put.description: Create or replace a semantic memory topic.  Topics should use lowercase slugs (e.g. ``users``, ``projects``, ``patterns``).

delete.description: Permanently delete a semantic memory topic.

get.description: Return all skills authored or forked by the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Authored Skills App V1 Intelligence Skills Authored Get

post.description: Create a new user-authored skill.  Supply the full SKILL.md content in the request body.  A ``skill_id`` is auto-generated if not provided.

get.description: Return the SKILL.md content for one user-authored skill.

put.description: Replace the SKILL.md content for an existing user-authored skill.

post.description: Create a copy of an existing authored skill under a new skill_id.  Use this to create a personal variant of a built-in or installed skill.
Parse and validate a SKILL.md document without persisting it.  Returns the parsed fields on success or a list of errors on failure.
Upload a SKILL.md file and store it as an authored skill.  The ``skill_id`` is derived from the ``name`` field in the SKILL.md frontmatter.  Returns HTTP 422 if the file is not a valid SKILL.md.

get.description: Return the saved canvas node positions and viewport state.

put.description: Persist the canvas node positions and viewport state.

post.description: Return ranked identity resolution candidates for a query string.

schema.title: Response Resolve User App V1 Identity Resolve User Post

post.description: Add a new alias to a resource or user node.

schema.title: Response Register Alias App V1 Identity Register Alias Post

post.description: Merge two duplicate resource nodes. keep_id becomes canonical.

schema.title: Response Merge Resource App V1 Identity Merge Resource Post

get.description: Read a policy file for the authenticated user's agent.

put.description: Write (create or update) a policy file for the authenticated user's agent.

get.description: Return all counterparties the authenticated user has conversation threads with. Reads the conversation index JSON from MinIO. (FR-UI-001)

schema.title: Response List Counterparties App V1 Conversations Get

get.description: List all channel/thread references for one counterparty. (FR-UI-001)

schema.title: Response List Threads App V1 Conversations  Counterparty Id  Get

get.description: Return messages in a single counterparty thread. Set reverse=true (default) to get newest messages first. (FR-UI-001)

schema.title: Response Read Thread App V1 Conversations  Counterparty Id   Channel   Thread Id  Get

get.description: Return all organisations the authenticated user belongs to (as owner or active member). (FR-UI-002)

schema.title: Response List User Orgs App V1 User Orgs Get

get.description: Liveness probe — always returns 200 when the process is alive.
Readiness probe — reports critical dependency readiness.
Readiness probe (alternative path) — checks broker connectivity.

post.description:
Accept a normalized inbound message and publish it to the broker queue.

Returns HTTP 202 Accepted immediately; downstream processing is asynchronous.

schema.title: Response Receive Inbound Api V1 Inbound Post

post.description:
On-demand trigger endpoint for ad-hoc agent activations.

Wraps the payload in an ``InboundMessage`` with ``channel="api"`` and
publishes it to the ``INBOUND_MESSAGES`` queue.

schema.title: Response On Demand Trigger Api V1 Trigger Post

get.description:
Handle Meta's webhook verification challenge (GET).

Meta sends a GET request with ``hub.mode=subscribe``,
``hub.verify_token=<token>``, and ``hub.challenge=<challenge>``.
We echo back ``hub.challenge`` if the token matches.

schema.title: Response Whatsapp Verify Webhooks Whatsapp Get

post.description:
Receive and process a WhatsApp Cloud API webhook event (POST).

Validates the ``X-Hub-Signature-256`` header before processing.
Returns ``{"status": "ok"}`` to Meta immediately; message processing
is asynchronous via the broker.

schema.title: Response Whatsapp Inbound Webhooks Whatsapp Post

post.description:
Receive a Telegram Update via webhook (POST).

Validates the optional ``X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token`` header
if ``TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET`` is set. Returns ``{"status": "ok"}``
immediately; message processing is asynchronous via the broker.

Only active when ``TELEGRAM_USE_WEBHOOK=true``.

schema.title: Response Telegram Inbound Webhooks Telegram Post

post.description:
Receive a Slack Events API callback (POST).

Verifies the ``X-Slack-Signature`` header before processing.
Handles Slack URL verification challenges transparently.
Returns ``{"status": "ok"}`` immediately; message processing is
asynchronous via the broker.

schema.title: Response Slack Inbound Webhooks Slack Post

post.description:
SES inbound email via Lambda → Gateway POST.

Accepts a JSON payload from the Lambda function that is triggered by
SES receipt actions. Verifies the HMAC-SHA256 ``X-GraphClaw-Signature``
header, downloads the raw email from S3, normalises it to an
``InboundMessage``, and publishes it to the broker.

Replaces IMAP polling in production (EMAIL_BACKEND=ses).
Local dev continues to use the IMAP poller (EMAIL_BACKEND=imap).

schema.title: Response Ses Email Webhook Webhooks Email Ses Post

post.description:
Receive a Microsoft Teams Bot Framework Activity (POST).

Returns ``{"status": "ok"}`` immediately; message processing is
asynchronous via the broker.

schema.title: Response Teams Inbound Webhooks Teams Post

A2AAgentEntry.title: A2AAgentEntry
A2AAgentEntry.description: A registered A2A agent (no plaintext key).

A2AAgentRegisterResponse.title: A2AAgentRegisterResponse
A2AAgentRegisterResponse.description: Response for POST /app/v1/a2a/agents — plaintext key disclosed once.

agent_name.title: Agent Name
agent_name.description: Human-readable name for the agent being registered.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional description of the agent's purpose.

callback_url.title: Callback Url
callback_url.description: Optional URL for platform-to-agent push notifications.

A2ARegistration.title: A2ARegistration
A2ARegistration.description:
Request body for ``POST /api/v1/a2a/agents``.

Attributes
----------
agent_name:
    Human-readable identifier for the agent being registered.  Must be
    unique per user; used in log entries and the agent list response.
description:
    Optional free-text description of what the agent does.
callback_url:
    Optional HTTPS URL the platform may use to push notifications back to
    the agent.  Currently stored for future use; not validated beyond
    basic string type.

params.title: Params
params.description: Method-specific parameters forwarded to the agent loop.

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional JSON-RPC request ID for client-side correlation.

A2AUpdatePayload.title: A2AUpdatePayload
A2AUpdatePayload.description:
JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope for inbound ``POST /api/v1/task-update`` messages.

Agents MUST send a JSON body conforming to this model.  The ``method``
field identifies the operation (e.g. ``"task.update"``, ``"task.complete"``).
The ``params`` dict carries the operation-specific payload and is passed
verbatim to the agent loop via the broker queue.

Attributes
----------
jsonrpc:
    JSON-RPC protocol version string.  Must be ``"2.0"``.
method:
    The JSON-RPC method name (e.g. ``"task.update"``).
params:
    Operation-specific parameters.  Forwarded as-is to the agent loop.
id:
    Optional JSON-RPC request ID for correlation.  Not used server-side
    for async processing but echoed in error responses when present.

ActionQueueItem.title: ActionQueueItem
ActionQueueItem.description: A single ranked item in the agent action queue.

AgentConfigSchema.title: AgentConfigSchema
AgentConfigSchema.description:
Typed runtime config for an agent (stored at {user_id}/agents/{agent_id}/config.json).

Fields introduced for canvas wiring:
- skills: IDs of installed skills this agent may invoke.
- mcp_servers: IDs of registered MCP servers this agent may call.
- tool_sets: Named tool-set IDs this agent can load_tool_set().
- sub_agents: Agent IDs this orchestrator may delegate to.

Secure-by-default: explicit empty list [] = no access.
Missing key (None) = all available (backward compatible).

GuardrailValidateResponse.description: Result of validating a rule set.

InboundMessage.title: InboundMessage
InboundMessage.description:
Normalized inbound message from any channel.

Attributes
----------
message_id:
    Unique identifier for this message. For email, sourced from the
    ``Message-ID`` header; for API/CLI, a caller-supplied UUID string.
channel:
    Origin channel. One of ``"email"``, ``"api"``, or ``"cli"``.
sender:
    Email address or user/agent identifier of the message originator.
subject:
    Email subject line or a short title for non-email messages.
body:
    Plain-text body of the message.
received_at:
    Timestamp at which the message was received by the gateway.
raw_headers:
    Original transport-level headers (email headers for email messages).
attachments:
    List of attachment filenames associated with this message.
session_id:
    Distributed tracing identifier in the format ``SES-{uuid4}``.
    Empty string when not yet assigned.
in_reply_to:
    ``Message-ID`` of the message this is a reply to, enabling threading.
    ``None`` for top-level messages.

JudgeStats.title: JudgeStats
JudgeStats.description: Aggregated judge statistics over a time window.

LLMProviderConfig.title: LLMProviderConfig
LLMProviderConfig.description: Configuration for a single LLM provider.

MCPAllowlist.title: MCPAllowlist
MCPAllowlist.description: Allowlisted MCP server URIs and trust tiers.

MCPApprovalOut.title: MCPApprovalOut
MCPApprovalOut.description: A pending MCP tool-call approval task.

MCPRegistrySearchResult.title: MCPRegistrySearchResult
MCPRegistrySearchResult.description: A result entry from the official MCP registry search.

MCPToolOut.title: MCPToolOut
MCPToolOut.description: A single tool exposed by an MCP server.

MemoryListEntry.title: MemoryListEntry
MemoryListEntry.description: One entry in a list of memory objects.

MemoryWriteRequest.title: MemoryWriteRequest
MemoryWriteRequest.description: Request body for writing a memory document.

OrgSummary.title: OrgSummary
OrgSummary.description: Lightweight org record for the OrgSwitcher UI. (FR-UI-002)

OutboundMessage.title: OutboundMessage
OutboundMessage.description:
Outbound message queued for delivery via any channel.

Attributes
----------
message_id:
    Unique identifier for this outbound message.
channel:
    Target delivery channel. One of ``"email"``, ``"api"``, or ``"cli"``.
recipient:
    Email address or user/agent identifier of the intended recipient.
subject:
    Subject line for email delivery; title for other channels.
body:
    Plain-text body of the message.
created_at:
    Timestamp at which the message was created and queued.
in_reply_to:
    ``Message-ID`` of the inbound message this is a reply to.
    ``None`` for agent-initiated outbound messages.
session_id:
    Distributed tracing identifier. Should match the ``session_id`` of
    the originating ``InboundMessage`` when applicable.

RebuildRequest.title: RebuildRequest
RebuildRequest.description: Optional body for rebuild — scopes the reconciliation to one org.

RightToErasureRequest.title: RightToErasureRequest
RightToErasureRequest.description:

Requires re-auth within the last 5 min (frontend passes re_auth_at).

modified_factors.title: Modified Factors

key_id.title: Key Id
key_id.description: Resource node ID of the agent registration.

plaintext_key.title: Plaintext Key
plaintext_key.description: New plaintext API key. Shown once — store securely.

rotated_at.title: Rotated At
rotated_at.description: timezone.utc timestamp when the key was rotated.

graphclaw__a2a__models__A2AKeyRotateResp.title: A2AKeyRotateResponse
graphclaw__a2a__models__A2AKeyRotateResp.description:
Response body for ``POST /api/v1/a2a/agents/{key_id}/rotate``.

The ``plaintext_key`` is shown exactly once.  The caller must store it
securely; subsequent calls to the rotate endpoint will invalidate this key.

Attributes
----------
key_id:
    The resource node ID identifying the agent registration.
plaintext_key:
    The new API key in ``wg_agent_*`` format.  Shown once; never stored.
rotated_at:
    timezone.utc timestamp when the rotation was performed.

graphclaw__api__admin__llm__LLMKeyReques.title: LLMKeyRequest
graphclaw__api__admin__llm__LLMKeyReques.description: Request body for storing an org-level LLM API key.

graphclaw__api__admin__llm__LLMKeyRespon.title: LLMKeyResponse
graphclaw__api__admin__llm__LLMKeyRespon.description: Response confirming an org-level LLM key was stored.

graphclaw__api__settings__LLMKeyResponse.title: LLMKeyResponse
graphclaw__api__settings__LLMKeyResponse.description: Response body confirming an LLM key was stored.

tags.name: health
tags.description: Liveness and readiness probes for orchestrators and load balancers.

tags.name: inbound
tags.description: Accept normalised inbound messages from any channel (email, API, CLI).

tags.name: outbound
tags.description: Queue outbound messages for delivery via email or other channels.

tags.name: triggers
tags.description: On-demand trigger endpoint for ad-hoc agent activations.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: OAuth 2.0 + Platform JWT authentication (login, callback, refresh, logout, me).

tags.name: a2a
tags.description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) REST API — agent key management (register, rotate, revoke, list) and the inbound task-update endpoint.

tags.name: webhooks
